behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure boundary is parser->hook-input handling handoff, not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess is required by the behavior text; in-process wiring is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI model involvement. Rejection-gate: System was considered and rejected because behavior says only "parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling" (no real process/OS requirement).
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test that feeds valid JSON containing code fences, backticks, and `$vars` through the same parser + hook-input handler modules used in production. Assert parse success, no parse-failure trace, and expected downstream handling state.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks parser error output plus trace-record construction/context fields across module boundaries. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real OS/process is not required to catch this failure; in-process malformed input reproduces it. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System was considered and rejected because the behavior only requires trace record content on malformed JSON, not real stdin/process lifecycle.
behaviors.test_description: In-process, submit malformed JSON cases (e.g., truncated object, unmatched quote) to the parser path wired to trace creation. Assert `json_parse_failed` is emitted and includes diagnostic context (offset/snippet/reason fields expected by schema).

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a cross-module contract (parse error -> policy mapper -> handled result + telemetry intent), not just parser logic. REAL-INFRA: explicitly "local error-policy path" disqualifies System as minimum. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System rejected based on "local error-policy path" text.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke local pipeline with malformed JSON so parse fails, then assert policy mapping returns handled outcome (no exception escapes) and telemetry intent flag/event is set as specified.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA dominates: behavior explicitly requires "real process execution" and malformed stdin handling plus exit behavior. MOCK-HIDE: in-process mocks can miss crash/exit-order/stdio edge cases. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow. Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is purely process I/O and tracing.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the actual hook executable as a subprocess, write malformed bytes/JSON to stdin, capture stderr/stdout/trace output, and assert: process exits without crash, `json_parse_failed` signal is emitted before termination, and exit code matches handled-failure policy.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)", which crosses process/stdin and sink integration boundaries. MOCK-HIDE: stubs can hide ordering/encoding/sink-write failures. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model decisions. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected by concrete text "real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink)".
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook through its real invocation entrypoint, provide malformed stdin payload, then verify the concrete trace artifact in the real sink format/location contains `json_parse_failed` with expected core fields.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a classification/attribution invariant across parser + error classification/trace modules using contrasting inputs. REAL-INFRA: no explicit real OS/process requirement, so System is not minimum. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI output dependency. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior is about distinguishing input classes, not real process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create paired in-process regression cases: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy strings, (b) malformed JSON. Run through shared parser+classification+trace path; assert only (b) emits `json_parse_failed`, while (a) follows success path with no malformed attribution.
